chunks.en: When something breaks, the first thing I do is check whether the change I just made caused it.
chunks.ja: 何かが壊れたとき、まず最初にするのは、直前に自分が加えた変更が原因かどうかを確認することです。

chunks.en: After that, I read the error message carefully and dig through the logs line by line.
chunks.ja: その後、エラーメッセージを注意深く読み、ログを一行ずつ丁寧に追っていきます。

chunks.en: Honestly, I tend to add print lines first, and only reach for the debugger when I'm really stuck.
chunks.ja: 正直に言うと、まずprint文を足しがちで、本当に行き詰まったときだけデバッガに手を伸ばします。

chunks.en: When I finally find the cause, I feel a huge sense of relief and can't help smiling.
chunks.ja: ついに原因を見つけたときは、ものすごい安堵感を覚えて、思わず笑ってしまいます。

outline: Opening: where I start when something breaks
Point 1: reading the error message and logs
Point 2: print lines first, debugger when stuck
Wrap-up: how relieved I feel when I find it

modelTalk.text: When something breaks, I don't panic right away. I try to remember what I changed last. Usually the newest bit of code is the guilty one. Then I actually read the error message, word by word. I used to skip it, but that's a rookie mistake. The logs often point straight at the line that's blowing up. If I'm still lost, I scatter print lines everywhere just to watch the values. It's a bit old-school, I know. Honestly, I reach for print far more than a proper debugger. Once I spot the real cause, it clicks into place like a puzzle. That moment feels amazing, almost like a weight lifting off. Sometimes it's something silly, like a typo or a wrong variable. Then I laugh at myself and shake my head. If I'd been more careful earlier, I'd have saved an hour. Still, catching it is such a relief.

chunks.en: When something breaks, the first thing I do is check whether it worked five minutes ago.
chunks.ja: 何かが動かなくなったとき、まず最初にやるのは、5分前は動いていたかを確認することです。

chunks.en: The first thing I do is read the actual error message instead of guessing what went wrong.
chunks.ja: 最初にやるのは、何が悪いのか当てずっぽうで考えるのではなく、実際のエラーメッセージをちゃんと読むことです。

chunks.en: To be honest, I usually just add a few print lines before I reach for the debugger.
chunks.ja: 正直なところ、デバッガに手を伸ばす前に、たいていはprint文をいくつか足すだけで済ませます。

chunks.en: When I finally find it, I feel this huge rush of relief, and honestly a bit silly.
chunks.ja: やっと原因が分かった瞬間は、ものすごい安堵感が押し寄せてきて、正直ちょっと自分がバカらしく思えます。

outline: Opening: what I check first when something breaks
Point 1: reading the error message and logs carefully
Point 2: my habit of print lines over the debugger
Wrap-up: how relieved I feel when I find the cause

modelTalk.text: When something breaks, honestly, the first thing I do is take a breath. I don't start changing code randomly — that usually makes things worse. Instead, I try to work out what changed last. Nine times out of ten, it's something I just touched. Then I actually read the error message, properly, not just skim it. To be honest, the answer's often sitting right there in the logs. That said, I do add print lines all over the place — it's a bit of a bad habit. I know I should use the debugger more, but stepping through feels slower to me. So I narrow it down bit by bit until I corner the culprit. And when I finally spot it? Oh, it's the best feeling. It's like a weight lifting off my shoulders. Sometimes I just laugh, because it was something silly all along. Then, of course, I quietly hope nobody saw how long it took me.
